bquelhas/subwaybuilder-map-opo.body:
# Porto (AMP) v0.2.1

## Fixes

- **POIs visíveis no mapa**: SYNTH_POIS (Torre dos Clérigos, Livraria Lello, Pavilhão Rosa Mota, Casa da Música, Coliseu, Mercado do Bolhão, Casa do Infante, Estádio do Dragão/Bessa, etc.) movidos da layer pois (que o renderer do jogo ignora) para 
eighborhood_labels/suburb_labels que SÃO renderizadas. Estratégia: min_zoom ≤ 11 → suburb_labels (landmarks regionais), ≥ 12 → neighborhood_labels (POIs detalhados).
- **Demand limpo**: removidos 450 pontos outliers que estavam fora da AMP (artefactos do clipping COS2018). Nearest-neighbor máximo agora é 2.74 km (era 152.8 km com j_025006 em Setúbal). Resolve demand_point_spacing validator check.

## Específicas

- Points: 48.485 | Pops: 59.476 | Pessoas: 1.19M | Cobertura ~69%
- Layers POI: `neighborhood_labels` agora tem 7+ landmarks injectados por tile no centro do Porto

## Estrutura do zip
Top-level uncompressed files (Railyard format).

bquelhas/subwaybuilder-map-opo.name: Porto (AMP) v0.2.0
bquelhas/subwaybuilder-map-opo.body:
# Porto (AMP) v0.2.0

Segunda release principal — modelo de procura mais granular usando dados oficiais.

## Novas fontes de dados

- **COS2018 v3 Série 2 (DGT)** — Carta de Uso e Ocupação do Solo, 83 classes hierárquicas, 25.343 polígonos clipped a AMP
- **PDM Porto 2021 (CMP)** — Carta de Qualificação do Solo Funcional, 717 polígonos categorizados (Espaços centrais, Atividades económicas, Equipamentos, etc.)
- OSM POIs e pontos manuais (preservados)
- INE BGRI 2021 (preservado)

## Modelo de procura aprimorado

- **30.957 locais de emprego** (vs 6.895 antes) — 4.5x granularidade
- **48.935 points** (vs 24.817) — 2x detalhe espacial
- **Drive-times density-aware** (D7): velocidade ajustada por densidade da subsecção de origem
    - Densidade > 8.000 hab/km² (centro Porto): carros 30% mais lentos
    - Densidade > 3.000 hab/km² (Boavista, Cedofeita): 15% mais lentos
    - Long-haul (>20km): mantém ~50 km/h efectivo para não inflar CP
- **Velocidade efectiva média 28 km/h** (vs 34 km/h antes) — mais realista
- **% viagens curtas <10min: 27.3%** (vs 35.7%) — carro perde competitividade no urbano
- Captive boost preservado (Porto x1.8, Gaia x1.6, Matosinhos x1.35, …)
- Cobertura: 69.1% (1.20M pessoas simuladas)

## Fix do validador integrado

O `points.residents = sum(pops.size por residenceId)` é agora aplicado no fim do pipeline — não precisa de script manual.

## Ficheiros (top-level no zip)

- `OPO.pmtiles` (44 MB) — basemap (inalterado vs v0.1.1)
- `config.json` (com version 0.2.0)
- `demand_data.json.gz` (1.89 MB) — modelo de procura novo
- `roads.geojson.gz`, `buildings_index.json.gz`, `runways_taxiways.geojson.gz`, `ocean_depth_index.json.gz`

## Notas

Os POIs sintéticos turísticos (Torre dos Clérigos, Livraria Lello, etc.) ainda não renderizam visivelmente — fica para v0.2.1 (investigação ao schema do renderer do jogo).

# Porto (AMP) v0.1.1

## Cobertura

## Procura modelada

## Mudanças desde v0.1.0-beta

## v0.7.0

### What's New

- **buildings_index.bin**: Added binary building index for new game version compatibility. `buildings_index.json` retained for old game versions — Railyard loads whichever format the game supports.
- **Cho-level allocation fixes**: Zero-resident port islands (Daikoku, Ogishima, etc.) and inland wards (Hodogaya, Shin-Yokohama) now correctly allocated from official cho-aza census data.
- **Full zoom range**: z7-z15 tiles retained.

| Demand points | 30,488 |
| Pops (flows)  | 277,098 |
| Max residents | 8,886 |
| Buildings | 936,786 |
| Pack size | 246 MB |

### Compatibility

- Game version: `>=1.0.0`
- Ships both `buildings_index.json` (old) and `buildings_index.bin` (new)

seanyushoumori/train-anarchy.name: Train Anarchy v1.0.0
seanyushoumori/train-anarchy.body:
First public release. Lifts Subway Builder's rolling-stock limits via toggles in the **Settings** menu, grouped by risk.

**Capacity & throughput** (on by default — useful, sim-stable)
- Any train length (1–100 cars)
- Exact car counts (no car-set snapping)
- Unlimited frequency (trains/hour)
- Instant station stops (no dwell)
- No station-approach slowdown

**Full anarchy** (off by default — extreme, feeds the physics sim)
- Unlimited top speed
- Instant acceleration & braking
- Huge passenger capacity
- Full-speed crossovers

Each toggle applies the relaxed value when on and restores that train type's vanilla default when off. Companion to **Track Anarchy** (which lifts the build limits). Verified on Subway Builder 1.4.x.

**Install:** download `train-anarchy-v1.0.0.zip`, unzip into your mods folder (`~/Library/Application Support/metro-maker4/mods/train-anarchy/`), and enable it in Settings → Mods.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.3.2 — realistic curve speeds by default
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body: Fixes 'every track runs at max speed'. The **Sharp curves** lever was also removing the per-curve speed limit; that speed boost is now its own **Unlimited curve speed** lever, **off by default** — so sharp curves are still buildable but trains slow down through tight ones like normal. Flip the new lever on if you want the old max-speed-everywhere behavior back.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.3.1 — road crossings at any elevation
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body: Follow-up to v1.3.0: track can now cross roads at **any elevation**, not just exactly at grade or deep/high enough. Fixes the ~-13ft to +16ft dead zone where a crossing was too shallow to tunnel and too low to bridge. Part of the at-grade road/highway/runway crossing lever.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.3.0 — highway, runway & ocean-floor crossings
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body:
Lifts three build limits that 1.4.x gates in collision indexes the official modding API can't reach (so the earlier flag-only fix wasn't enough):

- **Highway crossings** — the at-grade road-crossing lever now also lets track cross highways (retypes them in the road collision index; how highways *render* is unchanged).
- **Runway/taxiway crossings** — the same lever now lets track cross airport runways/taxiways.
- **Build below the ocean floor** — a new lever that lets track sit under the seabed without being elevated.

Also removes the per-road-class grade-crossing trains/hour cap. Each is a separate toggle; turn any off to return to vanilla. Built on 1.4.x.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.2.1 — fix stats not reverting after a soft reload
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body:
Fixes a bug where relaxed stats didn't revert to vanilla when a lever was toggled off after a soft reload (Cmd+R).

The game keeps modified train-type stats across a soft reload but the mod loses its captured defaults, so stats without a fallback (station lengths) stayed relaxed. This release persists the captured defaults (so revert survives a reload), self-heals the capture from any clean value, and ships a complete per-train fallback table as a last resort.

Requires 1.4.x's `allowGradeCrossing` handling from v1.2.0; backwards compatible with 1.3.x.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.2.0 — Subway Builder 1.4.x fix
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body:
Compatibility fix for Subway Builder 1.4.x (backwards compatible with 1.3.x).

- Fixes the at-grade road-crossing lever: 1.4.x renamed the train flag `allowAtGradeRoadCrossing` → `allowGradeCrossing`; the mod now sets both.
- The "Any track segment length" lever also lifts the new `MIN_SINGLE_TRACK_LENGTH` / `MIN_ADVANCED_TRACK_LENGTH` limits.
- Applies only the constants the running version actually has.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.1.1
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body: Adds a prominent reminder in the settings panel that the game must be restarted for changes to fully apply.

seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.name: v1.1.0
seanyushoumori/track-anarchy.body:
Selective anarchy. A new settings panel (in-game and on the main menu) lets you pick exactly which build limits to lift — curve
  radius, grade, clearances, parallel-track spacing, the build-height floor/ceiling, station/segment lengths, at-grade road crossings — 
  instead of all-or-nothing. Your choices persist across sessions, and toggling a limit back on cleanly restores the vanilla value. v1.0.0 
  lifted everything; v1.1.0 puts you in control.

seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.name: v1.2.2 — single-rail length + 1.4.x verified
seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.body:
- Parallel/quad builds now show a single rail's length (the distance actually being laid) instead of the summed length of all rails.
- Removed leftover debug logging.
- Verified compatible with Subway Builder 1.4.5.

seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.name: v1.2.1
seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.body:
Crossing-marker fixes: no more false same-height matches at stacked interchanges (e.g. −40 over −60 no longer flagged), every 
  same-height grid crossing now shows, plus a debounced recompute and O(N) overlay de-dup for smoother panning.

seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.name: Track Visualizer v1.2.0
seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.body: Same-height crossing markers (new toggle), per-toggle help tooltips, and a fix for doubled height labels on hot-reload.

seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.name: v1.1.1 — node-height performance + instant units toggle
seanyushoumori/track-visualizer.body:
- Existing-node heights are now cached and rebuilt only on real mutations (event-driven, with a cheap O(N) signature safety net) instead of every frame. 
  - Spatial-indexed elevation lookups + grid dedup → the "show built heights" toggle is O(N), no freeze on big maps.
  - Labels re-project only when the camera moves; idle frames do nothing.
  - Fix: units toggle now updates existing labels immediately.

hsullivan1201/sb-tod.name: v0.2.10
hsullivan1201/sb-tod.body:
## Fixes

- Long station names no longer render as the station id hex (`#xxxxxx`) in the TOD panel. Transfer-station groups whose names are joined with ` - ` (e.g. "Capitol Dr - Gateway - Roosevelt Grove - K") were tripping a name-length heuristic and falling back to the id. They now truncate normally like any other long name.

hsullivan1201/sb-tod.name: v0.2.7
hsullivan1201/sb-tod.body:
## What's Changed

- Preserves dirty, not-yet-saved TOD deals when the game fires a same-save load/reinit event.
- Prevents a just-funded build from being replaced by older persisted storage during that reinit path.
- Adds regression coverage and a smoke check for the same-save dirty reinit case.

## Notes

This debug dump did not show a TOD day tick, active deal apply, or persistence write in progress during the freeze snapshot. The mod state was idle and demand integrity was clean. The dump did reveal the same-save reinit deal-loss path fixed here.

## Validation

- `tsc --noEmit`
- `git diff --check`
- targeted TSX smoke test for dirty same-save reinit preservation
- esbuild bundle to `dist/index.js`

Note: local `vitest run` is still blocked by the existing Rolldown native binding/codesign issue on this machine.

hsullivan1201/sb-tod.name: v0.2.6
hsullivan1201/sb-tod.body:
## What's Changed

- Fixes a deal-loss bug where a just-funded development could be charged, appear as stored, then disappear when the live save name synced before the first day tick.
- Makes build confirmation always sync the current save slot before charging.
- Makes `addDeal()` wait for any in-flight save-name sync before accepting the deal.
- Allows only compatible, unapplied active deal-only payloads to follow a save-name sync, and recovers recent charged `_unsaved` orphan deals for empty named saves.
- Adds regression coverage for the save-name/deal-only handoff and the pending sync race.

## Validation

- `tsc --noEmit`
- `git diff --check`
- targeted TSX smoke test for deal save-sync, pending sync before add, and recent `_unsaved` orphan recovery
- esbuild bundle to `dist/index.js`

Note: local `vitest run` is still blocked by the existing Rolldown native binding/codesign issue on this machine.

hsullivan1201/sb-tod.name: v0.2.5
hsullivan1201/sb-tod.body:
## What's Changed

- Fixes a save-slot live-sync recursion loop that could flood debug traces and freeze after save renames or compatible slot recovery.
- Keeps normal gameplay lighter by making hot-path diagnostic payloads lazy and removing the always-on runtime interval sampler.
- Adds a regression smoke test around dirty old-slot persistence during save switching.
- Updates package, manifest, and bundle versions to 0.2.5.

## Validation

- `tsc --noEmit`
- `git diff --check`
- esbuild bundle to `dist/index.js`
- save-switch smoke test

Note: local `vitest run` is still blocked by the known Rolldown native binding/codesign issue on this machine.

ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.name: Regions v0.5.0
ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.body:
## v0.5.0 - 2026-07-22

_Game version_ v1.4.0

### New Features

- Added EE (Estonia) region support for:
  - Maakonnad (`ee-maakond` | counties)
  - Omavalitsused / linnaosad (`ee-omavalitsused` | municipalities / city districts)
  - Asustusüksused (`ee-asustusuksused` | settlement units)
- Added UA (Ukraine) region support for:
  - Райони (`ua-raions` | raions)
  - Громади / міські райони (`ua-hromadas` | hromadas / city districts)
  - Населені пункти (`ua-naseleni-punkty` | settlements)
- Added LV (Latvia) region support for:
  - Novadi un valstspilsētas (`lv-pasvaldibas` | municipalities)
  - Apkaimes un pagasti (`lv-apkaimes` | neighbourhoods & parishes)
- Added LT (Lithuania) region support for:
  - Savivaldybės (`lt-savivaldybes` | municipalities)
  - Seniūnijos (`lt-seniunijos` | elderships)
  - Gyvenamosios vietovės (`lt-gyvenvietes` | localities)
- Added PE (Peru) region support for:
  - Provinces (`pe-provinces`)
  - Districts (`pe-districts`)
- Added CN (China) region support for:
  - 区县 (`cn-districts` | districts)
  - 街道/乡镇 (`cn-subdistricts` | subdistricts)
- Double-clicking a region in the Info Panel's Commuters → Region breakdown (table row or Sankey node) now opens that region's info panel and focuses the
  map on it.

### Other Updates

- Added native-language region labels for EE, LV, and LT datasets, and bilingual (Ukrainian + English) labels for UA.
  - ![Latvia Example](img/latvia_example.png)
- Added local static recognition for downloaded EE/UA/LV/LT as well as PE/CN city datasets so release/distributed data can be discovered by the mod even though those countries are not supported by the runtime fetch wrappers (same as JP, CZ, PL, and TW).
- Added data scripts to validate external/collaborator archives, sanitize GeoJSON samples into canonical mod format, and add missing label coordinates.
  - For game versions 1.4.0+, the Regions Info Panel is now draggable
  ![Draggable Info Panel](img/draggable_info_panel.png)

### Bugfixes

- Fixed blank region labels: the map label font is now resolved at runtime
  against the fonts the game's glyph endpoint actually serves instead of requesting a
  fixed font the newer game build no longer provides.
- Hardened navbar host layer resolution such that the Regions Info Panel properly latches in versions 1.4.0+

ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.name: Regions v0.4.10
ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.body:
## v0.4.10 - 2026-05-24

_Game version_ v1.3.0

### New Features

- Added PL region support for:
  - Powiaty (`powiat` | counties)
  - Gminy (`gmina` | municipalities)
  - Rejony (`rejon` | statistical census tracts)
- Added TW region support for:
  - 鄉鎮市區 (`township` | townships)
  - 里 (`li` | villages)
- Released initial datasets for all twelve currently Polish cities with boundary data sourced from GUGiK PRG (powiaty + gminy) and GUS BREC (rejony).
- Released initial datasets for all six currently released Taiwan cities with boundary data sourced from MOI village/borough polygons and DGBAS/MOI population inputs.
- Added two new CZ city datasets: České Budějovice (CBS) and Liberec–Jablonec (LBC).

### Other Updates

- Added local static recognition for downloaded PL city datasets so release/distributed PL data can be discovered by the mod even though PL is not supported by the runtime fetch wrappers (same as JP and CZ).
- Added local static recognition for downloaded TW city datasets so release/distributed TW data can be discovered by the mod even though TW is not supported by the runtime fetch wrappers (same as JP, CZ, and PL).
- Added bilingual TW labels using the JP-style Traditional Chinese + newline + English pattern for region labels and dataset names.
- Cleaned up PL powiat display names: stripped the redundant `powiat` prefix word from every powiat label and normalised capitalisation so regional powiaty appear as `Bolesławiecki` and city-powiaty as `Warszawa` instead of `powiat bolesławiecki` / `powiat Warszawa`.
- Made runtime fetch CLI network failures debuggable: `fetch failed` errors now surface their underlying cause chain (DNS failure, connection refused, timeout, TLS error) and error codes (e.g. `ENOTFOUND`, `UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT`) in both the per-retry warnings and the final error, instead of an opaque `fetch failed`.

### Bugfixes

- Fixed runtime fetch CLI failing on US datasets (`counties`, `county-subdivisions`, `places`, `zctas`) with an opaque "non-JSON response" error after the US Census Bureau tightened ACS API key enforcement.
  - The fetch CLI now sends a Census Data API key on every ACS request, using a bundled project-owned key by default so most users don't need to do anything. Power users can override with their own key by setting `CENSUS_API_KEY` in their shell.
  - If the key (either bundled or user-supplied) is ever rejected, an actionable error now points at <https://api.census.gov/data/key_signup.html>.
- Aggregated CZ ZSJ-díl features into ZSJ-level features in the `zsj` dataset.
  - Statutory cities (Praha, Hradec Králové, Plzeň, Liberec, etc.) were previously shipping fragmented sub-ZSJ subdivisions (e.g. `Bavoryně díl 1` through `díl 5`) as separate map regions; the díly of each ZSJ are now geometrically dissolved into a single region.

nzh0172/sb-station-dots.name: [v1.4.0] Dot style, route code, compact mode
nzh0172/sb-station-dots.body:
New features for **station group dots**:
- Introducing Dot Styles, with 7 new dot styles inspired by transit maps around the world!
  - Traffic light: KL
  - Bubbly: Seoul
  - Tri-color: Seoul
  - Sleek: Beijing
  - Cycle: Guangzhou
  - Capsule: Tokyo
  - Wormy: Singapore  
- Use default dot style (white dot) when zoomed out, overriding other dot styles 

New feature for **station labels**:
- Toggle route icons and station names visibility
- Split route code from route name (for Capsule & Wormy)

New feature: **Route code**
- Apply route codes on supported dot styles (Capsule, Wormy)
- Currently route code are only applied on station groups, as each station group dots have distinct properties than normal dots
- To give a route code, use this default format when renaming a route: [alphanumeric characters] [route name]
  - Only the first space matters for splitting
    - ie: PY Putrajaya Line (PY: route code, Putrajaya Line: route name)
  - Set custom delimiter for route code, any ASCII symbol is fine (default: first space)
    - ie: PY_Putrajaya Line, PY-Putrajaya Line
  - Normalize station number for station code (default: not normalized)
    - A station code consists of route code + station number (auto-configured)
    - ie: PY1 (default) --> PY01 (normalized to tenth place)
    - ie: 31 (Line 3, first station) --> 301 (normalized to tenth place), 3001 (normalized to hundredth place)
- Toggle station numbering on/off on route code 
- Known issues: Circular route station numbering is not working at the moment
- Route code is an optional feature, it will not affect your current station names, it only applies on capsule and wormy transfer dots.

UI improvements:
- **Compact mode**: Customization panels are now organized in tabs, and the main mod panel is smaller on compact mode
- Reset button is now red and placed on the header

nzh0172/sb-station-dots.name: [v1.3.0] Station labels update
nzh0172/sb-station-dots.body: New features added for **station labels**: - Sort route icons by text in ascending & descending order - Group route icons by shape - The grouping order is circle > square > diamond > triangle - Grouped route icons also can be sorted alphabetically by enabling above. - Adjust wrap width for route icons - Suitable for long route names! - - Join distinct station names with a slash '/' for station groups - For example, if a station group has 22 St and 23 St stations, it will show as 22 St/23 St - You can choose to preserve the joined station group name when zoomed out - You can also flip the joined names too - Joined names also dynamically changes on rename - The sorting order of the station names is according to the _list in station group panel_ (manual sort station names is not supported due to how the game handles the order itself) - Another example of Plaza Rakyat/Merdeka interchange in KL: - - [FIX] Flicker on hover at station dot labels replaced with snappier hover - [UP] Bumped the minimum base game version requirement to v1.3.0+ for future compability updates

mpfk/subway-builder-mode-manager.name: Mode Manager 1.1.2
mpfk/subway-builder-mode-manager.body:
Compatible with Subway Builder version 1.3.0

### Save load reliability & cross-mod compatibility

Reliability and compatibility release. Five user-reported bug fixes, all touching how Mode Manager interacts with Subway Builder's save load lifecycle, station placement engine, and other installed mods.

## Fixed

- **Toolbar disappears after exit-to-main-menu** (#11) — the Mode Manager toolbar button now correctly re-registers when you exit to the main menu and re-enter a save. Previously required a full game restart to recover.
- **Save load crash via Resume / Continue** (#12) — saves that use Mode Manager modes now load reliably from the main menu's Resume button. Synchronous train-type registration in `onCityLoad` reads the pending save name from `sessionStorage` to register before host deserialization.
- **Save load crash via Main Menu → Load/Save → pick save** (#12) — a separate host code path that skipped the sessionStorage write the Resume flow uses. A capture-phase DOM click interceptor on the host's save picker pre-stages the pending save name so the same `onCityLoad` registration completes in time. Both common save-load UI flows are now covered.
- **Foreign-mod ID conflicts no longer silently overwrite** (#14) — when a Mode Manager library mode shares an ID with another mod's registered train type, the panel surfaces the conflict with a 🚫 indicator and tooltip. `lockUsed` self-heal also skips foreign-owned IDs defensively, and `commitMode` rejects the commit with a clear error.
- **Spurious "Route uses mode X" console warnings** (#18) — `lockUsed` self-heal now filters host built-in train types (`heavy-metro`, `light-metro`, `commuter-rail`), eliminating the wall of warnings on saves built primarily with host built-ins.
- **Aerial Gondola and single-car bus modes wouldn't place stations** (#21) — station length formula updated to scale buffers proportionally with car size and respect the host's documented 10m placement floor. Built-in mode min/max station values updated. Single-car modes now have a 10m placement window; multi-car modes get more.

## Changed

- **Train-type registration is now fully synchronous** at `onCityLoad`, eliminating the async promise-chain race against host save deserialization.
- **Auto-computed `maxStationLength` formula** replaced. Previously a flat `⌈maxCars × carLength × 1.10⌉` that produced sub-pixel placement tolerance for small-cabin modes. Now: `max(⌈maxCars × carLength + carLength × 0.5 + 10⌉, minStationLength + 10)` — half-car proportional overhang on both endpoints plus an absolute 10m placement-slack on max only, with a window-preservation guard.
- **Auto-computed `minStationLength` formula** updated to add the same half-car overhang on top of the train's minimum length, floored at the host engine's 10m hard minimum: `max(⌈minCars × carLength + carLength × 0.5⌉, 10)`.

## Added

- Storage layer gained sync variants (`getSync` / `setSync` / `deleteSync` / `keysSync`) and the registry gained `getLibrarySync` / `getCommittedSync` helpers used by the init hot path. Async wrappers remain for UI / event-handler code.
- `external` issue label for tracking work that depends on action outside this repo.

## Known Limitations

- **Lazily-registering foreign mods may still cause save load crashes** ([#15](https://github.com/Mpfk/subway-builder-mode-manager/issues/15), closed not-planned). Some mods register train types lazily per-city instead of in `onGameInit`. If your save crashes with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'stats')` and you have other train/transit mods installed, the issue is likely on the other mod's side — ask its author to register train types in `onGameInit`.
- **Two undocumented host internals are load-bearing for the save-load fix** ([#17](https://github.com/Mpfk/subway-builder-mode-manager/issues/17), closed not-planned). The mod reads `sessionStorage["loadingSaveStats"]` and observes the save-card DOM contract from Subway Builder 1.3.0. Both fail loudly (clear console messages) if a future host update changes them.

## Upgrading from 1.1.1

⚠️ **If you have Aerial Gondola, Local Bus, Trolleybus, or BRT committed to an existing save**, the new station length values don't apply automatically — the snapshot-at-commit guarantee means existing snapshots stay pinned for save stability. To pick up the fix:

1. Open Mode Manager → **This Save** tab
2. Click **✕** next to the affected mode to remove it
3. Wait for the reload
4. Click **+ Add** to re-commit with the new values

All other fixes apply automatically on first launch.

## Installation

Download **mode-manager.zip** below, unzip it, and copy the files into your Subway Builder mods directory.

| Platform | Mods folder |
|---|---|
| Windows | `%APPDATA%\metro-maker4\mods\` |
| macOS | `~/Library/Application Support/metro-maker4/mods/` |
| Linux | `~/.config/metro-maker4/mods/` |

Then enable **Mode Manager** in **Settings → Mods** and restart the game.

mpfk/subway-builder-mode-manager.name: Mode Manager 1.1.1
mpfk/subway-builder-mode-manager.body:
Compatible with Subway Builder version 1.3.0

First public release.

## Added

- **Seven new built-in modes**: Local Bus, Trolleybus, Light Rail, Aerial Gondola, Funicular, High-Speed Rail, and Maglev — bringing the bundled total to 11 transit modes.
- **Cross-mode track sharing**: bus modes can use each other's roads; trams can run on light-rail tracks and trolleybus overhead-wire infrastructure.
- **In-game mode editor**: create new modes or edit existing ones via a form-based UI with section grouping, validation, and per-field error highlighting. Optional sections (Track Geometry, Maintenance Costs, Elevation Multipliers) collapse by default.
- **Mode actions in the Library tab**: per-row Edit (✏️), Duplicate (⎘), and Copy JSON (📋) buttons; bottom-of-list **Explore**, **Export**, **Create**, and **Remove Modes** buttons.
- **Auto-computed defaults** in the editor: blank fields show `auto: N` or `default: N` placeholders; on Save, station lengths derive from car count × car length, deceleration from acceleration + 0.1, and optional stats fall back to sensible defaults.
- **10% tolerance buffer** on auto-computed `maxStationLength` so placed stations comfortably fit the longest train without engine rounding rejecting them.
- **Mod-aware detection**: the This Save tab lists transit modes registered by other mods with a 📦 icon and "Managed by another mod" tooltip.
- **Import-failure recovery**: a failed import opens the editor pre-filled with whatever parsed, highlighting the specific validation issue inline.
- **Project copyright attribution**: explicit copyright header in `LICENSE` and `SPDX-License-Identifier` declaration in source.

## Changed

- **Library tab restructured** with **Saved Modes**, **Available defaults**, and **Import a Mode** sections.
- **Toolbar panel widened** to 480 px to accommodate the editor's two-column form; the panel also scrolls vertically inside the viewport.
- **Friendly editor labels**: stats display as Title Case (e.g., `maxLateralAcceleration` → "Max Lateral Acceleration (optional)").
- **Source-tag subtitles removed** from the Library — built-ins and imports are functionally identical from 1.0.4 onward.
- **README** rewritten with feature cards, screenshots, and a curated mode showcase.

## Fixed

- **Parallel and quad track build modes** no longer crash on custom modes — added the missing geometry stats (`parallelTrackSpacing`, `trackClearance`, `minTurnRadius`, `minStationTurnRadius`, `maxLateralAcceleration`, `maxSlopePercentage`, `stopTimeSeconds`, etc.) to BUILTINS and the auto-default layer.
- **Workflows on private repos**: `actions/checkout` failures resolved by declaring `contents: read` permission in `repo-setup.yml` and `labels-sync.yml`.

## Removed

- Verbose route-shape debug log that fired on every save load.

## Installation

Download **mode-manager.zip** below, unzip it, and copy the files into your Subway Builder mods directory.

| Platform | Mods folder |
|---|---|
| Windows | `%APPDATA%\metro-maker4\mods\` |
| macOS | `~/Library/Application Support/metro-maker4/mods/` |
| Linux | `~/.config/metro-maker4/mods/` |

Then enable **Mode Manager** in **Settings → Mods** and restart the game.

martig7/induced-demand.name: Induced Demand v1.1.0
martig7/induced-demand.body:
A ground-up rebuild of how induced demand grows: a transit-access field drives
where demand appears, new demand points are placed by subdividing existing ones.
Dense cities subdivide a little, but sparse cities subdivide a lot.

## New demand model: access-field infill

- Directional access from transit reachability: Residential demand keys on
  reachable jobs, commercial on reachable residents, computed over a
  route-aware station graph with schedule-derived wait/ride weights.
- Per-city density fit: induces pops realistically to the city's density.

## New points via Voronoi subdivision

- Demand points now materialize by subdividing the Voronoi cells of existing
  demand. Split pressure scales with how under-subdivided a cell is.

## Density-differential induction

- Population-density headroom gate on splitting: a cell stops accruing split
  pressure where local density already meets a target, so dense cities add few new
  points while sparse ones subdivide.
- Job agglomeration: job-dense land grows more jobs and gets a bigger cap, so
  a few centers concentrate instead of jobs spreading evenly.
- Tuned towards 20-25% induced growth on high density, ~50% on medium density, ~75% on low density.
  If you add a lot of access to an empty area, it can densify a lot further though.

## Overlay & display

- Access views: A continuous field that shows network access as a proxy for location attractiveness.
- Cells view:  Voronoi cells colored by split pressure which update daily.

## Performance

- Performance not too dissimilar from previous versions,
  with the caveat that every day at midnight the game slows for ~50 ms.

martig7/induced-demand.name: Induced Demand v1.0.4
martig7/induced-demand.body:
- Gave induced pops an equivalent commute time distribution to in-game pops
- Gave induced pops realistic routing and driving times

Induced pops used to leave home at exactly 8:00 and work at exactly 17:00, and their
driving distance and time were a flat estimate that ran 16 to 30% slow, which pushed
them onto transit more than comparable in-game pops. They now draw departures from the
game's own time of day distribution, and their driving is routed on the city's real
road network with speeds fitted per city, landing within about 1% of the game's own
distances and times. Clicking an induced pop draws its real road route.

Existing pops are repaired automatically on load.

- Pops orphaned by a city data update no longer break the commute simulation

martig7/induced-demand.name: Induced Demand v1.0.3
martig7/induced-demand.body:
- History: the panel lists the last 14 days of pop additions and removals; click a day to map them
- The game's own demand dots now update as induced demand changes
- Pops are retired safely on load; fixes endless "Pop not found" tick errors freezing game forever. They are additionally "removed" in real time (edited to be sized zero, removed from pop lists)
- Only stations served by routes induce demand

martig7/induced-demand.name: Induced Demand v1.0.0
martig7/induced-demand.body:
First release! This mod models long-run induced demand by adding demand pairs to well-served nodes.

martig7/improved-schematics.name: Improved Schematics v2.3.0
martig7/improved-schematics.body:
# Improved Schematics v2.3.0

## Highlights

Two new station designs, route specific options, custom themes, and many algorithmic improvements.

## What's new

**Station designs**
- Two new station-marker designs: London and Toronto.

**Route Menu**
- Simplify individual routes: Draw specific routes thinner, gray, and/or dashed. Or just remove them!

**Theme & colors**
- The map now tracks your in game theme, and can be focused to light or dark mode if desired.

**Crop & reframe**
- You can now crop the map to whatever limits you'd like, which gives the canvas more room to draw lines cleanly.

**Layout & warp controls**
- Geography warp is now a toggle, and is off by default.
- Box warp split into two dials: Declutter and Aesthetic emphasis. Declutter is genuinely necessary in many cases so it is on by default at half power.
- Line-size control: thin or thicken all lines and markers together.

**Labels**
- Labels now rotate and split to avoid overlaps.

**Geographic mode**
- Interchanges are drawn as circles instead of ovals.

## Under the hood

- Capsule megaboxes are removed
- Draw overhaul that should generally improve quality/consistency.
- Bundles now overlap completely, so you can't see one bundle behind another.

martig7/improved-schematics.name: Improved Schematics v2.2.0
martig7/improved-schematics.body:
# v2.2.0

## Highlights

Stations can now be drawn in your choice of marker style, including several
transit-inspired numbered designs. Maps can also label neighborhoods based on game data,
plus a few map polish fixes.

## What's new

### Choose your station design

- **New "Station design" picker**: pick how station markers are drawn. Applies instantly with a repaint.
- Includes six designs to start: **Classic** (the original dot), **NYC-Solid**, **NYC-Map**,
  and three numbered-marker styles inspired by Japanese railways: **Tokyu**
  (numbered squares), **Tokyo** (JR-style numbered boxes), and **Tokyo Metro**
  (ringed numbering discs).
- This comes with a new capsule type for Japanese station designs, and allows for a lot more designs in the future.

### Neighborhood labels

- Neighborhoods can now be embedded in the map, and you can customize the neighborhoods shown in settings.

### Geographic station size

- The station size slider now works in geographic mode (it didn't work before, oops).

## Fixes & polish

- Closed circular routes now join cleanly at the seam instead of leaving a
  gap.
- Fewer loop/spike artifacts and stray slivers at dense hubs, plus a range of
  seating and merge correctness fixes (fold stubs, lane spacing, service-ladder
  welds, course windows).
- The panel now survives the game's UI reloads without losing the toolbar
  button.
- "Split station complexes" is now "Split station groups" and defaults to
  off. You should enable split station groups if you have extremely large station groups.

martig7/improved-schematics.name: Improved Schematics v2.1.0
martig7/improved-schematics.body:
# v2.1.0

## Highlights

Maps render far cleaner, box warp is a lot more consistent, hubs are automatically split, and there are
a lot more options for the geography render.

## What's new

### Box warp is directional & multi-layered

- Box warp detects which direction would be more optimal to expand and chooses that one,
which allows for more effective expansion at a cheaper cost.
- Large warps get decomposed based on density.

### Cleaner stations & interchanges

- **Improved capsule overlap detection**: capsules that would collide are
  pushed apart, with a seat-time check + retry and move guards.
- **Split station complexes**: a large multi-line interchange can render as
  separate markers instead of one giant box (on by default, but reversible).
- **Better marker placement**: a rewritten seating "escalation ladder" seats far
  more stations cleanly (far-attach corridor capsules, best-effort seating for
  split platforms) instead of falling back to boxes. It also does this more efficiently
  than the previous escalation ladder.
- **Curve hubs**: an over-dense bundle that still can't seat draws as a soft
  curved blob instead of a hard rectangle (now the default, boxes were the default before).

### Cleaner bundle interchanges

- **Rewritten line ordering** ("bundle-blocks"): lines within a bundle are
  ordered with a rigid block model that produces fewer crossings and keeps
  straight runs stable. This enforces bundle twists at 90 degree corner or
  bundle split/joins. Same bundle twists only occur as a last resort.

### Map framing & backdrop

- **Maps adopt the game's orientation**: the layout is rotated into each city's
  default map bearing.
- **Landmass styling**: the simplified land/water backdrop can be drawn as
  rounded or octilinear "blobs". The simplification is warp-aware (dense-core
  geography is preserved), and enforces a "no-lake creation policy" (the algorithm
  would've otherwise created artificial lakes).

### Fixes & polish

- Loop / spike artifacts at dense hubs reduced.
- Pipeline greatly simplified with the goal of generalizing as many fixes as possible.

martig7/improved-schematics.name: Improved Schematics v2.0.0
martig7/improved-schematics.body:
## What's new in v2.0.0

A major release. The renderer was rebuilt on an interactive canvas, gained re-simulated
detail-area popouts, a box-based density warp that declutters crowded hubs, and instant save/load backed by
a fingerprint-gated layout cache, all tunable from a new settings panel.

### Interactive canvas renderer
- The map now paints to a hardware-accelerated **canvas** instead of an SVG/DOM tree: smooth,
  coalesced pan and zoom, faster redraws, and far lower memory on dense networks.
- A Scene-IR pipeline emits the drawing directly (no SVG re-parse), with analytic label-overlap
  handling. Vector SVG export is unchanged.

### Detail areas (magnifier popouts)
- Draw a box on the map to spawn a persistent, colour-coded popout that **re-simulates just that
  region**. This declutters a crowded interchange into its own readable mini-map.
- Multiple areas at once; each is draggable, wheel-zoomable, and lockable (pin it and pan/zoom the
  whole map through it).
- Manage areas (rename, recolour, delete) and edit their bounds with draggable corner handles.
- The main map cuts out the selected region and draws leader lines to the popout; labels under a
  box are culled. Areas follow the Stations/Labels toggles and the label-size setting.
- Areas are baked into PNG/JPEG/SVG exports and saved/loaded with the map.

### Decluttering: density & box warp
- A **density warp** gives crowded hubs more room while keeping the far field geographically
  faithful (a fold-free 2D Gastner–Newman field composed with a dense-box expansion).
- Tunable from Settings: **Geography warp**, **Line accuracy**, **Box warp** (local hub
  magnification, with an expanded range), and **Box density cutoff** (how many / how large the
  warped regions are).
- **Dense-hub fallback:** an un-seatable interchange no longer has to be a hard rectangle — a new
  **Hubs: Box / Curve** toggle can draw it as a smooth capsule instead.
- A **Warp boxes** overlay toggle visualises exactly which regions the box-warp magnifies.

### Save / load and instant reloads
- **Save / Load map** to a file: a full snapshot (layout, settings, detail areas, sub-layouts)
  that restores instantly.
- A **fingerprint-gated layout cache** skips the expensive layout recompute when your network and
  settings are unchanged, so reopening or regenerating is near-instant (a "Cache used" note shows
  when it hits). Cache reads are 14–148× faster, and fresh generation is faster too.
- A **Clear cache** button wipes the current city's cached layout while keeping your settings.

### Geography backdrop
- Water and parks are harvested in the background per city and **persisted** (extent-aware), so the
  backdrop appears immediately on later opens.

### Settings & export
- A settings popover with appearance sliders (line thickness, station size, map margin, label
  size) plus the realism/box sliders, staged behind **Save** with **Reset to defaults**.
- **Export** to SVG, PNG, or JPEG with resolution and quality controls.
- **Per-mode visual settings**: geographic and smoothed each remember their own look. The panel
  always opens in geographic mode; the heavier smoothed map is generated on demand.

### Reliability & polish
- A **loop line** the game models as two directions (same name + colour over the same track) now
  draws as a single ribbon instead of two parallel ones while a genuine **branch** (a trunk that
  splits to different terminals) stays distinct and draws as a proper Y.
- Many warp, marker, label, and geography-harvest fixes. Edge-terminus disconnections, capsule/box
  stability, world-space (constant) label sizing, and harvest-timing races.
- Lucide icons throughout, top-bar popovers kept inside the panel, and a version marker in the panel.

martig7/improved-schematics.name: Improved Schematics v1.2.0
martig7/improved-schematics.body:
## What's new in v1.2.0

### Complete Deterministism
The schematic layout is now bit-for-bit deterministic, which makes problems a lot easier to debug between the game and my dev tools.

### Cleaner station markers
- **Interchange bullets no longer overlap** — rings inside a capsule are scaled below the spacing floor so the colors stay separate.
- **Compact capsules** that hug their actual line bundle instead of stretching across lines that don't stop there, and a placement solver that now avoids unnecessary turns.
- **Mutual capsule slide** resolves overlaps one-sided sliding couldn't.
- **Terminus stops** anchor to their real lane, so split-node stations stay compact (fixes stations that could previously vanish).
- **Nameless routes** show a clean blank dot instead of a raw route id.


### Stronger geographic warp
The density warp that spreads out crowded areas is more aggressive by default and now responds to local station crowding, giving dense downtowns more breathing room while keeping the map true to real geography.

### Panel & display polish
- Fit-to-view and SVG export now frame on the map's water/green geography extent instead of the padded canvas.
- Loading spinners on label/station re-renders and the async geography harvest.
- New **Waypoints** icon for the panel and menu button.
- Geographic mode no longer renders U-bracket transfer staples between nearby unconnected stations.

martig7/improved-schematics.name: Improved Schematics v1.1.0
martig7/improved-schematics.body:
## What's new in v1.1.0

### Tile-derived geography backdrop
Improved Schematics now renders **water, coastline, and parks** behind your transit map based on the game's map tiles.

### Other improvements
- **Modded-map support** reads green from the `landuse` layer for maps using a custom tile schema.
- **Smoothed map persists** across mode switches and panel close, with a new **Regenerate** button next to Labels.
- Cleaner geography: proportional size filtering, smoothing, hole-filled parks, merging of road-split park fragments, and fixes for tile seams / clip artifacts.

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.0.0 "Phoenix"
danield1909/dantrains.body:
Welcome to the release of danTrains 1.0.0 "Phoenix"!

There's been a lot of changes since the last version so here's a broad summary changelog:
- danTrains is now a standalone mod with no other requirements. This was done primarily due to the outdatedness of addTrains.
- Three new menus have been added, two as pop ups in game and one on the main menu. The instructions will explain how to use them.
    - Panel 1 - Train Registration: Allows you to register trains in game
    - Panel 2 - Train Dictionary: Allows you to search through all the train types in more detail in game,
    - Panel 3 - Main Menu Save Loader: Allows saves to not break
- Perth, Australia has been added with 3 trains thanks to Pio from the SubwayBuilder discord
- Tags have been directly made visible
- danTrains has been added to Railyard (unless you were like super quick to this in which case I may be doing it rn)
- Mod structure has been changed to allow easier updating of train data which should provide faster train updates in the future
- Automation, Electrification, Track Gauge, and Loading Gauge are all things you can now choose for all trains when adding them
    - In the future this will affect track compatibility but the game cannot handle that yet
- Train speed on curves has been adjusted to match real life standards (based on track geometry but converted to lateral acceleration bc that is what the game uses)
- All costs have been set to approximate Mediterranean prices
    - I was not able to find good sources on my own so I will admit I asked ChatGPT for Italian prices and then did some scrutinizing
    - If you have better sources let me know
    - Train cost per hour is a very American metric as in Europe they do cost per km per passenger or just cost per km
    - This has basically universally lowered prices which does make the game easier but it is what everyone voted for
    - Commuter Rail is still very expensive tho. Because it is in real life.
- Spreadsheet has not been updated and is no longer a priority due to the existence of the in-game dictionary
- Lateral acceleration limits no longer affected by automation. 
- Added 1.2.1 beta compatibility for the new station length feature.
- Once registered, descriptions now include track type and creator. Trying to figure out the best way to do tags.
- Continents have been replaced with Regions since continents are big and hard to define while regions are smaller and slightly easier to define
- Train Dropdown is now typable inside of so you can just start typing and find what you want.
- Various other minor things I cannot remember at this exact moment 

PLEASE NOTE: NEW FEATURES ARE A LITTLE FINNICKY BUT NOTHING SHOULD BE GAMEBREAKING. I OFFER NO GUARANTEES THOUGH, JUST MY EXPERIENCE.

INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Registering trains:
    1. To register a train you need to open the registration tab once you are in a game and ensure Automation, Electrification, Track Gauge, and Loading Gauge are all set
    2. Click Preview
    3. Review the stats and see the description and also make sure your train lengths are right
    4. Click register to register
    5. Check to make sure it worked
    6. Save the game with a specific name so you can use it in the future (THINGS DO NOT SAVE WITHOUT AT LEAST AN AUTOSAVE, AND AN AUTOSAVE WILL OVERWRITE ANY OTHER AUTOSAVES)
2. Loading Saves:
    1. Locate the big panel in the main menu that should have "Dan Trains" in it
    2. Navigate the dropdown to the save you want to play.
    3. Press the white button
    4. Load your save as normal
3. Managing Save Data:
    1. If you want to delete any data, go to the panel at the front.
    2. Select the save data you want to delete from the dropdown.
    3. Press the red button.
    4. As a failsafe, you need to press a second (formerly dark red, now red) button
    5. If you click on the dropdown you should see that it is gone.
4. IMPORTING LEGACY SAVES:
    1. First, make sure the trains are DanTrains Gen 1 Trains and not from some other mod. I cannot import those ones.
    2. Open SubwayBuilder with the following mods enabled:
        1. [DanTrains Gen1 (0.3.1) (NEW VERSION)](https://github.com/DanielD1909/danTrainsGen1/releases/tag/Final)
        2. addTrains DT (comes with 0.3.1 DanTrains)
        3. DanTrains 1.0.0 Phoenix
    3. Open the game with the correct trains enabled via addTrains.
    4. Save the game (probably with a new name just in case)
    5. Exit to menu and then disable danTrains 0.3.1 and addTrains DT
    6. Open the game
    7. Follow Loading Saves instructions

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: v0.8.6 | Complete redesign!
gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.body:
Rebuilt the Railway Overlay into a route-aware real-world transit layer with improved rendering, controls, performance, and custom-map support.

Added Realistic and Infrastructure map modes with support for metro, subway, light rail, tram, monorail, commuter rail, regional rail, intercity rail, platforms, yards, construction, proposed, and abandoned infrastructure.

Added global route-colour detection using OSM, GTFS, GeoJSON, recognised network palettes, multilingual colour names, and per-map custom overrides.

Improved shared-corridor rendering so urban transit colours take priority while heavy and commuter rail remain neutral when sharing the same tracks.

Collapsed duplicate route relations, reversed geometry, directional variants, repeated route-master data, and matching parallel tracks.

Merged stop positions, platforms, station nodes, and nearby duplicates into unified station complexes with interchange symbols and route-colour rings.

Added Route Inspector with route highlighting, station lists, shared-service information, route statistics, and clear-selection controls.

Added visibility controls for individual lines, networks, service families, and transport types.

Added local classification overrides for incorrectly tagged routes and a Data Health panel for missing colours, broken geometry, duplicate data, and incomplete level information.

Added Blueprint comparison mode for viewing real alignments and stations against the player’s constructed network.

Moved the dashboard to Subway Builder’s native top-toolbar panel and restored a compact Map, Visibility, Data, and Settings layout using native UI components.

Added Automatic, Low, Balanced, and Maximum performance profiles with geometry simplification, zoom-based detail, station thinning, cached processing, debounced updates, and lightweight source filtering.

Reworked track-level detection to calculate Surface, Underground, Elevated, and Unspecified states per individual segment instead of across an entire route.

Improved underground detection using tunnel, underground-location, and negative layer or level tags.

Improved elevated detection using bridge, viaduct, elevated-location, and positive layer tags.

Removed the incorrect assumption that every metro or subway route is fully underground.

Added failure-safe rendering so partial layer errors automatically fall back to a stable overlay instead of leaving broken or incomplete map layers.

Added IndexedDB persistence, automatic migration of older imports, import history, rollback support, and separate settings for every built-in or custom map.

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: v0.2.2 | Performance Update!
gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.body:
- Debounced build-action resync triggers so duplicate hooks only schedule one resync pass per action.
- Moved OSM v6 parsing to a Web Worker path with automatic fallback to main-thread parsing if worker processing fails.
- Optimized station coverage checks using grid-bucket spatial partitioning instead of full cross-product distance scans.
- Reduced unnecessary map layer reorder work by checking current layer order before issuing moveLayer calls.
- Reduced style-driven reapply pressure by only queueing overlay resync when core overlay layers are actually missing.
- Added lifecycle cleanup for worker and timers on game-end reset to avoid stale background work.

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: V0.2.0 | Major | Semi-Stable

Major update - redesigned overlay panel with layer toggles, persistent IndexedDB cache restored, faster and more reliable layer refresh after build/style changes, improved platform and station mode detection (including London Underground tagging edge cases because they are tagged funny), and stronger top-layer rendering behavior.

Haven't tested on all maps, only maps tested: NYC + LDN, happy for testers.

Four new real-time metrics in the Route panel:

stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.name: v1.3.1
stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.body:
Recalibrate load-factor thresholds to reflect real world scenario.
A route averaging 50% across the day likely hits 80–90% at rush hour,
which is healthy service, not "underused".
The healthy green band is now 40–80%, and only routes below 20% are
flagged red as under-served.

New thresholds:
- CRITICAL_LOW 40→20,
- WARNING_LOW 55→40,
- CRITICAL_HIGH 90→95.

stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.name: v1.3.0
stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.body:
New Features & Metric Refinement
- Load Factor Metric: Introduced a new analytic metric to track passenger density and vehicle capacity utilization across your subway lines.
- Hourly Data Aggregation: All major metrics (including Load Factor) are now tracked on an hourly basis, allowing you to identify peak congestion times and troughs throughout the day-night cycle.
- Performance Metric (formerly "Usage"): Transformed into a dimensionless multiplier measuring how much ridership a route generates relative to its bidirectional throughput capacity.

Check the "User Guide" for all the details

UI & Visual Enhancements
- System Statistics Dashboard: Refined the GaugeArc design for a cleaner, more readable visual representation of network health.
- Navigation: Updated the PanelBreadcrumb design to improve menu navigation and layout consistency.
- Tooltips: Overhauled tooltip designs

Technical Improvements & Bug Fixes
- LifeCycle Management: Fixed a logic error in lifecycle timing by implementing strict greater-than (>) comparisons.
- Optimized data processing by fully adopting the Accumulator Pattern, eliminating redundant logic and improving performance during high-traffic periods.
- Code Cleanup: Removed deprecated files and legacy logic that were superseded by the new accumulator architecture.

https://ahkimn.github.io/subwaybuilder-t.changelog: Map pack update.

Rendered landuse now resolves a wider OSM greenery palette (forest, wood, grass, meadow, cemetery, orchard, nature reserve, etc.) into the in-game park umbrella, so far more parkland, forest, and natural cover renders than in 0.1.x. Added hospital, port, convention / exhibition center, stadium and arena, and cultural-center demand. Multi-campus universities are now split across their campuses, attraction coverage expanded, and resident coverage restored for newly created villages. **0.2.1**: track-collision now applies to small streams, ponds, and irrigation lakes (previously only large rivers / reservoirs registered). Cleaner mountain rendering — subterranean canals (anti-silt tunnels, power-plant diversions, culverted irrigation) are no longer drawn on the surface, and admin-boundary nature reserves / national parks no longer paint green over the settled villages inside them. Landuse and water now align at every zoom level so coastlines no longer show thin overlap artifacts.

Worker totals and rural coverage come from the CZSO 2021 census employment measure with sub-settlement commute O/D, and a gravity-based correction brings the COVID-inflated 2021 self-commute back to plausible pre-pandemic levels. Special-demand coverage spans hospitals, universities, airports, museums, theatres, libraries, spa/wellness, and sports & convention venues.

https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ryandi.changelog: Metro-scale rebuild. Expanded the map from the city core to the full OKC metro (Edmond, Norman, Moore, Del City, Midwest City, Yukon, Bethany, and Warr Acres); roughly a 59 x 67 km bounding box (up from ~32 x 29 km). Buildings regenerated from Overture Maps (2026-06-17) release via depot: 455,904-entry collision index built from 678,989 Overture footprints (filtered to ≥120 m² for the index and ≥50 m² for visual tiles), replacing the older sparse multi-source build with dense residential coverage across the whole metro. `buildings_index.bin` for SB 1.4.x compatibility alongside `buildings_index.json` for older versions. Demand fully regenerated from US Census LODES 2022 origin-destination commute data over the metro with DEMAND_FACTOR raised from 1 to 5 and driving routes computed via OSMnx: total modeled demand grows from 235,101 to 2,163,635 across 1,886 demand points and ~71,000 commute OD pairs, with residents, jobs, and pops fully reconciled and no empty points. Special demand for Will Rogers World Airport (OKC), seven universities (OU Health Sciences Center, UCO, OCU, OSU-OKC, OCCC, Rose State, Mid-America Christian), entertainment anchors (OKC Zoo, State Fair Park, Bricktown), and Tinker AFB. Multi-scale city/suburb/neighborhood labels rebuilt natively, with downtown districts added: Downtown, Bricktown, Deep Deuce, Midtown, Arts District, and Automobile Alley.
Added new buildings_index.bin for 1.4.x compatibility. Added missing buildings from index to pmtiles for correct rendering behavior.

Added city, suburb, and neighborhood labels across the OKC metro bbox. Thinned neighborhood labels from 67 to 25 features by deduping OSM plat artifacts and enforcing a 600 m minimum spacing between kept features; survivors include Bricktown, Midtown OKC, Wheeler District, Heritage Hills East, Asian District, Mesta Park, and Jefferson Park. Added country:US metadata to config.

Initial release. 378,004 buildings from five sources (Overture, OKC City 2020 aerial servey, FEMA/ORNL, Microsoft, OSM). High-quality LODES demand data with pre-calculated routes. Includes Will Rogers World Airport (OKC), 7 universities, Tinker AFB, and entertainment venues.
